content.problemStatement:
Salmon has some hidden numbers that he wants you to find!

Given an integer NNN, find and output **any** two integers A and B such that:


1≤A,B≤1091 \le A, B \le 10^91≤A,B≤109, and
A∗B=NA*B = NA∗B=N.
###Input:
The first line of input consists of a single integer TTT (1≤T≤1051 \le T \le 10^51≤T≤105) -- the number of testcases.

The next TTT lines will consist of one integer, NNN (1≤ N≤1091 \le N \le 10^91≤ N≤109).

###Output:
For each of the TTT testcases, output two space-separated integers AAA and BBB.

###Subtasks


Subtask 1 [100 points]: No additional constraints.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10^5), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10^9).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output two space‑separated integers A and B (1 ≤ A, B ≤ 10^9) such that A·B = N. Print each pair on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each N we output the trivial factor pair (1, N). All pairs satisfy 1 ≤ A,B ≤ 10^9 and A·B = N.

solution.approach: Because N ≤ 10^9, the pair (1, N) always satisfies the constraints (1 ≤ 1 ≤ 10^9 and 1 ≤ N ≤ 10^9). For each test case we simply output "1 N". This runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time operation per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
